zotero.abstractNote: Movement primitives are a powerful tool for representing complex robot behaviors. However, sequencing these primitives to achieve long-term goals remains a challenge. In this paper, we propose a framework for learning attribute grammars that can represent the hierarchical structure of complex tasks and the dependencies between movement primitives. Our approach combines the structural flexibility of context-free grammars with the continuous state representation of attribute grammars. We demonstrate the effectiveness of our method on a variety of robotic manipulation tasks, showing that it can learn to sequence movement primitives to achieve complex goals while maintaining robustness to variations in the environment.
